novel_features.name: Cross-site arbitrage scan
novel_features.description: Find products where one comparator's lowest offer beats the other by at least your gap threshold.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a synced cross-source product join keyed on EAN with fallback to normalized title — neither site offers this view.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent needs to identify dropshipping or discount opportunities by comparing the two indexes; only this CLI has the cross-source lowest-per-site computation.

novel_features.name: Stock-aware best-of-both lowest
novel_features.description: Cheapest current offer for a query across both sites, optionally filtered by stock and shipping cap.
novel_features.rationale: Requires querying both sources' offers and computing MIN(price + shipping) over the joined set with stock filter — no single-site call returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Pick this when a user names a product and asks for the best price right now; one call returns the unified answer instead of an agent juggling two calls.

novel_features.name: EAN cross-link
novel_features.description: Resolve an EAN to both Prisjakt and PriceRunner product IDs and return the union of current offers.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local product table with EAN columns sourced from both sites; neither comparator exposes the other's product key.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when the user has an EAN/GTIN from a manufacturer page or barcode scan and wants to short-circuit name-based search.

novel_features.name: Catalogue diff per category
novel_features.description: Products that appear in one site's category index but are missing from the other.
novel_features.rationale: Requires set-difference SQL across both sources' category-tagged product tables after a fresh sync — only possible with a unified local store.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this to spot niche Swedish merchants or rare SKUs only one comparator indexes.

novel_features.name: Cross-site price-drop digest
novel_features.description: Watched products whose latest snapshot is at least your percentage threshold below the snapshot at the start of the window.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a price_snapshot time series populated by repeated sync runs joined to a tracked_product list — no comparator surfaces drops across both sites in one query.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use as the daily or weekly digest when an agent is reporting deal flow on a user's wishlist.

novel_features.name: Cross-site watchlist with thresholds
novel_features.description: Track products by ID or EAN with a maximum price; check whether any tracked item is at or below threshold across either site.
novel_features.rationale: Requires a local tracked_product table joined against current offers from both sources — no single comparator unifies bookmarks across the two sites.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when an agent maintains a user's shopping list and surfaces threshold-met items without checking each site separately.

novel_features.name: Unified cross-site price history
novel_features.description: Merged time series of price snapshots from both sources for one product, resolved via EAN.
novel_features.rationale: Requires merging snapshots tagged per source on a stable product key — the brief explicitly notes neither site exposes the other's history.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Use when answering trend questions or producing graphs that need both sites' historical observations.

novel_features.name: Sale-season anomaly check
novel_features.description: Compare the current price against the local 90-day median to flag sticker-stuffed sales.
novel_features.rationale: Requires window-aware median over the price_snapshot table — pure local statistics, no API call returns this.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Reach for this when a Black Week or summer-sale claim looks suspicious and you want a mechanical answer.

narrative.headline: Combined CLI for Prisjakt and PriceRunner with cross-site arbitrage, price-drop digests, and EAN-keyed lookups no single-site tool offers.
narrative.value_prop: se-prices syncs both Swedish price-comparison aggregators into a local SQLite store and exposes commands neither site can: `arbitrage` finds gaps between the two indexes, `lowest` returns the cheapest in-stock offer across both in one call, `history-combo` merges price-snapshot time series, and `watchlist` cross-checks your bookmarks against both sources. Per-site commands stay first-class so agents can drill in when needed.
narrative.auth_narrative: No authentication required. Both sites are read from their public SSR HTML state. Prisjakt's search route clears its Cloudflare challenge via Surf with a Chrome TLS fingerprint; everything else uses plain stdlib HTTP with a browser user agent.

quickstart.comment: Verify reachability for both sources and inspect the local store.
Populate the local store from one category on both sites — fastest path to a useful local index.
Cross-site cheapest in-stock offer in one call — the headline workflow.
Find products where one site undercuts the other by at least 10% — ranked by absolute SEK saved.
Track an iPhone product across both sites; the next `watchlist check` reports whether either site offers it at your max.

troubleshoots.symptom: Prisjakt search returns 403 or a Cloudflare "Just a moment" page.
troubleshoots.fix: Run `se-prices-pp-cli doctor` to confirm Surf transport is active for Prisjakt search; the CLI auto-selects Surf for that route. If it still fails, set `SE_PRICES_TRANSPORT=surf` to force the Chrome TLS fingerprint.
Run `se-prices-pp-cli sync --source both --category <slug>` first; the local store is empty until you sync at least one category from each site.

troubleshoots.symptom: Cross-site match not found for a known product.
troubleshoots.fix: Inspect the EAN with `prisjakt product <id> --select ean` and `pricerunner product <id> --select ean`; if either side is missing the EAN, fall back to `lowest "<product name>" --fuzzy-name`.
PriceRunner blocks raw curl-style user agents on `/results`; the CLI sends a real browser UA by default. If you've set `SE_PRICES_USER_AGENT` to something terse, unset it.
Snapshots are written by `sync` and the watch commands; schedule `se-prices-pp-cli sync --source both --watched-only` on a cron to keep `drops`, `is-sale`, and `history-combo` accurate.

narrative.when_to_use: Pick se-prices when an agent needs to compare Swedish retail prices across both Prisjakt and PriceRunner. It is the right tool when the user names a product, EAN/GTIN, or category and wants the lowest cross-site offer, an arbitrage gap, a merged price history, or a wishlist threshold check. Skip it for non-Swedish retail and for live realtime checks beyond the most recent sync — the analytical commands read the local snapshot store, not the live APIs.

recipes.title: Tonight's lowest in-stock iPhone offer across both sites
recipes.explanation: Cross-site min price filtered by stock and shipping cap; --select narrows the response so the agent doesn't pay for the full ProductList payload.

recipes.title: Daily Black Week arbitrage sweep on TVs
recipes.explanation: Surfaces only ≥8% gaps worth at least 500 SEK; pair with a daily cron to catch Black Week and Mellandagsrea price moves.

recipes.title: Wishlist check across both sites
recipes.explanation: Returns each tracked product's best current cross-site offer plus a boolean for threshold-met items.

recipes.title: Is the current Black Week price actually a sale?
recipes.explanation: Compares current price against the 90-day median from the local snapshot store; flags sticker-stuffed sales as `actually_a_sale: false`.

recipes.title: Unified 60-day price history for an EAN
recipes.explanation: Merges price snapshots from both sources into one time series, CSV for spreadsheet plotting; works only if both sites have synced the EAN at least twice in the window.

trigger_phrases: compare prices on prisjakt and pricerunner
watch this product for price drops in sweden
is this a real black week deal
